opening_template: Start with a public collision at a donor-backed midnight campus run, then reveal the two leads are tied to an old homophobic attack and a flash drive labeled with the school's buried abuse archive.

hook_distribution.launch: public rumor, false-couple setup, and evidence teases
hook_distribution.rise: chemistry spikes, buried abuse clues, and family pressure escalations
hook_distribution.storm: betrayal, public hearings, and physical danger to Cade and June
hook_distribution.payoff: live exposure, institutional collapse, public choice, and emotional thaw

season_hook_map: 0%-20%: why is the donor-family golden boy protecting the exact evidence that could destroy his life
20%-45%: is the fake relationship turning real before the truth is safe
45%-80%: who buried the old assault archive and what will Cade sacrifice to keep June alive
80%-100%: can the truth go public before the system rewrites the story again

episode_hook_map.hook_out: Ezra opens June's dropped flash drive and sees a folder labeled DISCIPLINE ARCHIVE.
A board hearing summons both Ezra and Cade over the viral collision rumor.
Cade agrees to fake-date Ezra in front of a student camera.
June vanishes right after warning Ezra not to trust anyone inside athletics.
Pastor Mercer recognizes the Quinn surname and realizes Ezra is Noah Quinn's brother.
An audio file proves Coach Brennan ordered players to stay silent after the old assault.
Ezra gets a text warning him to drop Cade before Noah's sealed file is released.
Instead of denying the rumor on stage, Cade takes Ezra's hand under the donor lights.
June's camera catches the almost-kiss neither lead can explain away anymore.
Noah returns to campus right after Ezra and Cade finally kiss for real.
The flash drive disappears before Noah can review it.
Cade is suspended when their private kiss leaks online.
June shows up bruised and says her father found the backup drive.
Pastor Mercer accuses Ezra of kidnapping Cade.
Coach Brennan offers Cade his future back if he lets Ezra take the fall.
Cade stays silent at the hearing and Ezra sees the text that explains why: They have my sister.
Brock corners June at the rink before the handoff is complete.
A live mic captures Pastor Mercer threatening June by name.
Cade tells Ezra he loves him before the truth is safe enough to hold.
Ezra publishes without Cade's consent, and Pastor Mercer's people drag Cade home.
Ezra reaches the Mercer house and finds Cade bloodied on the church steps.
The athletic director releases altered footage that makes Cade look like the aggressor.
The arena scoreboard glitches as Noah's hidden helmet-cam file starts to play.
Final episode resolves through public truth, public choice, and a public kiss.

paywall_map.beat: The Mercer father realizes the boy near his son is the victim family's surviving witness.
Ezra and Cade kiss for real, then Noah returns before they can define what it means.
Cade appears to betray Ezra at the hearing, but the hidden text reveals June is being held over him.
The arena screen begins playing the master proof just before the championship faceoff.

rhythm_curve.launch_phase: viral collision, fake relationship, and first archive reveals compress the setup into public action instead of routine campus encounters
rhythm_curve.rise_phase: chemistry and evidence develop in parallel while the father, coach, and rumor machine squeeze tighter
rhythm_curve.storm_phase: hearings, forced silence, June's danger, and Cade's apparent betrayal create rapid alternating pain and payoff beats
rhythm_curve.payoff_phase: hospital, custody, public testimony, arena reveal, and emotional confession land in quick succession

satisfaction_mix.status_reversal: Each public pressure event ends with Cade reclaiming agency from the school, the father, or the rumor machine.
satisfaction_mix.romantic_tension: Fake dating, near misses, secret care, and one mistimed confession keep emotional appetite high.
satisfaction_mix.truth_drop: The archive is released in fragments, forcing viewers to chase the next missing file, missing witness, or altered clip.
satisfaction_mix.family_escape_payoff: June's safety becomes the emotional proof that love here creates a future, not just chemistry.

villain_ladder.face: Brock Danner, the locker-room loudmouth and rumor weaponizer
villain_ladder.pressure: public humiliation, violence bait, and leaked clips
ice time, scholarship leverage, and loyalty blackmail
family terror, church image, and direct abuse
evidence suppression, legal spin, and official narrative control

cultural_swap_rules.target: public donor event, athletics hearings, and media-space collisions
cultural_swap_rules.reason: Short-drama structure needs a more external and public conflict engine.
Creates stronger hooks and stronger cliffhangers without losing the romance core.

cultural_swap_rules.target: private healing plus public testimony and institutional takedown
cultural_swap_rules.reason: Overseas short-drama audiences convert better on visible reversals and system-level pressure.

cultural_swap_rules.source: family house confrontation only at the end
cultural_swap_rules.target: family, church, school, and donor spaces braided throughout
cultural_swap_rules.reason: Keeps external stakes active across the whole season.

role_function_map.screenplay_function: sharp-tongued driver of deals, reveals, and reversals
role_function_map.setting_bible_function: defines the cold editorial visual language

role_function_map.screenplay_function: body-in-danger lead whose choices move every public beat
role_function_map.setting_bible_function: defines the sports prestige and damage visual language

role_function_map.screenplay_function: direct abuse, control, and moral hypocrisy pressure source

role_function_map.storyboard_function: motion and panic accent in cold frames

role_function_map.screenplay_function: legitimizes Cade inside the hockey world
cuts exposition with practical action and emotional truth
role_function_map.setting_bible_function: adds color and intelligence to the campus support system

forbidden_traps: long internal monologues replacing visible action
ending with private closure only and no public reversal
adaptation_summary: Rebuild the source into a premium queer campus scandal romance. Keep the cold-to-thaw emotional engine, abusive father pressure, hockey masculinity, and found-family payoff, but move the plot into a fake-dating cover, buried athletics abuse archive, live-hearing conflict, and public takedown structure designed for 24 addictive short episodes.

season_arc.goal: Create the false-couple engine and establish the buried archive as the real prize
Let chemistry and evidence deepen together while external pressure hardens
Explode trust through hearings, coercion, leaks, and family danger
Deliver hospital survival, public proof, institutional collapse, and romantic answer

scene_language_rules: Keep scenes built on accusation, negotiation, protection, or confession. No idle campus chatter.
Every scene must shift leverage, release a clue, or intensify intimacy.
Make public scenes sharper and shorter than private scenes.
Let Cade speak in clipped defense and Ezra in precise provocation until both soften.
Use hockey, church, and media spaces as visual pressure machines rather than background decoration.
similarity_avoidance: Rename every major character and rebuild the supporting cast around a donor-board scandal structure.
Replace Brown versus Providence daily-life rhythms with a single Catholic hockey campus, media rooms, hearings, and donor events.
Turn the emotional core into a fake-dating plus buried-archive engine rather than a mostly routine slow burn.
Move the climax from a direct replay of the original house rescue into a public church-step capture, custody battle, and arena proof event.
Write wholly original dialogue and rearrange confession, betrayal, and reconciliation timing.

similarity_score.assessment: The adaptation preserves only the core emotional engine: an icy queer sports romance thawing under family abuse pressure. Names, setting container, plot chain, conflict spaces, reveal order, and climax mechanics are substantially rebuilt below the 30-point threshold.
